Human Owner design decisions: hard dependencies are satisfied only by done; task reparenting is forbidden in MVP; epic.key is immutable after task refs exist; locks/resources are postponed unless required by TIG-05; legacy TASK-XXX remains resolvable during compatibility period.

payload.notes: TIG-02 reviewed and accepted. Epic key support implemented; EPIC-004 assigned TIG. Existing generated drift in CODEX_PROMPT.md and DOCS_GAPS.md accepted as pre-existing and not blocking this task.

TIG-04 reviewed and accepted. Central task reference resolver implemented with TASK-XXX backward compatibility.

payload.reason: Architecture design depends on the current ctl inventory.
ID allocation strategy depends on the current ctl inventory.
Core package implementation depends on the approved architecture design.
Core package implementation depends on the global ID allocation strategy.
Command registry depends on shared core services.
Unified CLI facade depends on command registry metadata.
Compatibility wrappers depend on the unified CLI/service facade.
Project doctor depends on the unified CLI facade.
Locking and atomic writes depend on shared core services.
Read-only Web Control Center should surface doctor health output.
Read-only Web Control Center should follow locking and atomic write protection foundation.

Owner quickstart needs stable aictl interface details.

payload.notes: Inventory report accepted. Findings are sufficient for control-plane architecture design.
Unified control-plane architecture accepted. Design is sufficient for ID allocation strategy task.
Global ID allocation strategy accepted. Identity, ref, UID, alias, locking, validation, and parallel allocation rules are sufficient for core package design.
ai_project_ctl core package foundation accepted. Existing ctl behavior preserved; tests and validation passed.
Command registry foundation accepted. Registry is metadata-only, existing ctl behavior preserved, tests and validation passed.
Unified aictl facade accepted. Facade delegates to existing ctl scripts, registry discovery works, existing ctl behavior preserved, tests and validation passed.
Legacy ctl compatibility wrapper migration accepted. Shared plumbing extracted safely; domain behavior, command syntax, lifecycle gates, protected-file rules, and anti-recursion boundaries preserved.
Project doctor diagnostics accepted. PASS/WARN/FAIL model implemented; hard validation delegated to owning CLIs; stale prompt/context artifacts correctly reported as warnings.
Locking and atomic write protection accepted. FileLock, atomic writes, audit append fsync, tests, and compatibility checks passed.
Read-only local Web Control Center MVP accepted. UI is loopback-only, GET/HEAD-only, generated files are labeled derived, and no web mutation paths were added.
Controlled Web write actions accepted. All writes route through aictl/registered commands, require confirmation, preserve loopback-only safety, and do not directly edit protected files.

payload.text: Human Owner approved refreshing context/Codex generated execution artifacts for CTL-12 closure only, through owning CLIs. No source behavior changes are allowed.

payload.notes: Owner quickstart and centralized control-plane documentation accepted. aictl, web control center, doctor, controlled web writes, task refs, context/prompt flow, generated-output rules, and protected-file rules are documented.

payload.notes: Web Control Center performance optimization accepted. Dashboard and data endpoints no longer run full doctor on every request; doctor refresh remains explicit; write safety preserved.

payload.notes: Task workflow automation MVP accepted. Workflows compose governed commands, require confirmation, stop on blocking failures, and preserve owner gates.
Evolution Change Wizard accepted. It creates and prepares Change Proposals through governed commands, links tasks correctly, and preserves Human Owner approval gates..
Task Creation Wizard accepted. Create-only workflow uses governed taskctl/aictl paths, supports dependencies, does not auto-start tasks, and preserves protected-file boundaries.
Bulk Task Import UI accepted. Import is JSON-based, preview-first, validates refs before creation, requires confirmation, and creates tasks only through governed command paths.
Review and close helpers accepted. Workflows preserve Human Owner confirmation, lifecycle gates, linked Change completion rules, and reject invalid closure.

payload.notes: Unified action result panel accepted. Web actions now render a human-readable result panel with step status, warnings/errors, changed/generated files, next actions, Codex handoff instruction, and hidden/redacted technical details.
Task Review Done Controls accepted. in_review tasks now expose Approve & Done and Request Changes with required confirmation and owner notes, routing through governed workflows.

Evolution management UI tab accepted. Web Control Center now supports Change Proposal listing, filters, metadata, create-for-task, approve, move-to-review, and accept actions through governed workflows.

payload.text: Evolution tab layout issue observed after WFA-10 acceptance: Change Proposals table does not fit practical viewport width. Long fields such as affected files and risks should be summarized in the table and moved into collapsible details.
Evolution Change Proposal rows remain readable in normal viewport widths by using compact summaries, collapsed details, or horizontal overflow protection.
Long affected files, risks, impacts, and metadata do not make the main Evolution table unusable.

WFA-14 needs changes. In in_review state, Approve & Done can fail on stale contextctl.check-generated, but the UI marks Refresh Context unavailable because task status is in_review. Also Request Changes was blocked by protected.check, which prevents owner review feedback from being recorded. The hint/action model should expose safe repair actions and Request Changes should not be unnecessarily blocked by stale generated/protected context artifacts.

payload.notes: WFA-14 accepted. Next-action/blocker hints now show actionable repair paths for stale in-review context, and Request Changes is no longer blocked by protected-file/generated checks.

WFA-15 accepted. Codex Execution Report Submission implemented and verified. Structured task reports can now be submitted through governed CLI paths, stored in task_reports state/events, exposed to the Web read model, and do not replace Human Owner review.

WFA-16 accepted. Task Review Package View implemented and verified. UI now shows task metadata, linked Change status, latest Codex report, changed/generated files, checks, warnings, blockers, notes, and governed review controls without bypassing Human Owner gates.

payload.text: WFA-17 accepted. Current Execution panel implemented and verified. UI now shows current task identity, Context Pack/Codex prompt readiness, generated paths, copyable Codex instruction, and governed refresh/clear actions while preserving protected-file boundaries.

payload.notes: WFA-17 accepted. Current Execution panel implemented and verified. UI now shows current task identity, Context Pack/Codex prompt readiness, generated paths, copyable Codex instruction, and governed refresh/clear actions while preserving protected-file boundaries.

WFA-18 accepted. Project Health Repair Actions implemented and verified. UI now exposes doctor summary, generated artifact health, docs render, context/Codex refresh, protected-file checks, confirmation-gated repair actions, and safe-target rejection through governed command paths.
Extend Bulk Task Import to support uploading JSON files in addition to pasted JSON text.

payload.notes: Define the supervised batch pipeline automation policy model and safe presets.
Select the next executable task from an owner-selected queue under policy constraints.
Persist supervised pipeline sessions, selected queue, policy snapshot, step status, stop reason, and audit references.
Implement a supervised run-next pipeline action that advances exactly one safe step and stops on blockers.
Gate Codex execution using the actual prompt payload token budget and policy thresholds.
Launch or hand off Codex Executor only after policy and Token Budget Gate PASS, then capture execution metadata.
Validate Codex structured execution reports before review and closure gates.
Run deterministic machine checks for tests, doctor, protected files, generated outputs, allowed_files, token usage, and blockers.
Run a narrow read-only Codex Reviewer prompt for semantic review and structured verdict.
Apply policy-controlled close/rework decisions only after Machine Review PASS and Codex Review APPROVE.
Create local commits only when policy allows and commit readiness is green.

payload.title: BUG-01 Fix Approve & Done stale execution context handling

payload.notes: Run repeated pipeline run-next steps until first blocker, policy violation, unsafe condition, token failure, or queue completion.

payload.text: Changes requested. TASK-067 made contextctl.check-generated non-blocking, but Approve & Done is still blocked by protected.check when protected-check reports only prompt/context drift: OUTDATED_GENERATED_FILE CODEX_PROMPT.md and CODEX_PROMPT_RENDER_FAILED STALE_CONTEXT_PACK. The close workflow must treat prompt/context freshness failures inside protected.check as warning-visible and non-blocking, while keeping real protected-file violations blocking. Current behavior still forces Refresh Context before owner acceptance, so option B is not fully implemented.

payload.text: Allow local_command to include explicit Codex sandbox/approval flags, for example a safer owner-configured command such as codex exec --sandbox <mode> when supported by the installed Codex CLI.

Add a preflight diagnostic that detects Codex sandbox startup failures such as bubblewrap/user-namespace errors and reports them as CODEX_ADAPTER_SANDBOX_UNAVAILABLE instead of generic local_command_nonzero_exit.
Document how to verify the local Codex CLI command manually before using it in executable pipeline.
Do not hardcode danger-full-access; require owner-configured policy/command allowlist for any weaker sandbox mode.
When codex exec fails because bubblewrap/user namespaces are unavailable, adapter reports a clear sandbox compatibility blocker.
Owner can configure the local Codex command/allowlist to use a Codex CLI sandbox mode supported by the local environment.
Adapter still refuses any command not present in command_allowlist.

Support owner-configured Codex sandbox mode through local_command, including commands such as codex exec -s workspace-write, codex exec -s danger-full-access, or explicitly allowed bypass mode when the environment is externally sandboxed.
Do not hardcode danger-full-access or bypass mode in the adapter; allow only owner-configured commands that are present in command_allowlist.
Default prompt transport must pass CODEX_PROMPT.md to codex exec through stdin.
Detect Codex sandbox startup failures, including bwrap, loopback, RTM_NEWADDR, Operation not permitted, user namespace, or bubblewrap errors, and report them as CODEX_ADAPTER_SANDBOX_UNAVAILABLE.
Add diagnostics that include bounded stderr/stdout snippets for failed local Codex commands without storing full prompt text.

Document manual preflight commands for local Codex execution: codex exec -s workspace-write < AI_PROJECT/generated/CODEX_PROMPT.md and codex exec -s danger-full-access < AI_PROJECT/generated/CODEX_PROMPT.md.
Adapter passes CODEX_PROMPT.md content to codex exec through stdin by default.
Owner can configure local_command and command_allowlist to use codex exec -s danger-full-access when workspace-write sandbox is unavailable.
Adapter refuses any Codex command that is not present in command_allowlist.
When Codex fails with bwrap/loopback/user-namespace sandbox errors, adapter reports CODEX_ADAPTER_SANDBOX_UNAVAILABLE instead of generic local_command_nonzero_exit.
Adapter stores only bounded stdout/stderr snippets and hashes, never full prompt text.
Tests cover stdin prompt transport, sandbox failure detection, allowlist enforcement, and non-zero stderr diagnostics.

Manual check showed codex exec supports sandbox modes: read-only, workspace-write, danger-full-access, plus --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox. PIPE-26 must keep weaker modes owner-configured and allowlisted, not hardcoded.
